hero.question: Will Connecticut win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 13.4%, the market is pricing Connecticut as a fringe tournament team with almost no shot at a title, which means you're getting paid pennies to believe in a realistic Final Four run. The Huskies have real talent and a path to a 1-seed if they execute, making this a classic case where the crowd's recency bias has overcorrected on a program that could absolutely make a run.

daily_take.headline: Connecticut's 13% tourney odds look like free money if you know the roster
daily_take.deck: Connecticut barely moved today, but the flatness masks real action underneath. The Huskies have a legitimately elite recruiting class coming in and a proven coach in Dan Hurley who just delivered a natty at UConn in 2011. At 13.4%, you're pricing in near-zero improvement and ignoring that this program has institutional DNA for tournament success. Sharp money would nibble here.
